Feral Hogs: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver (HBO)
LastWeekTonight

Feral Hogs: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver (HBO)

更新於: 2026-06-26 18:23 ▶ 原片
@xXxHolyBigMacxXx ❤️ 8.9k
Background for people who don’t know:


The head writer (Daniel Obrain) for John Oliver worked for Cracked at the same time as his good friend Cody Johnston. Cody Johnston host a show called here’s some more news. Where he goes deep into political/news discussions in a comedic way. Every April fools day he does the “Boar report” and talks about how feral hogs are trying to take over the world.

How many times do you think Cody pitched this Daniel before he said yes?
@Coolmark0128 ❤️ 5.6k
Cody Johnston freaking the f out 😂
@benbriggsmusic ❤️ 4.4k
Happy to see the comments understood the assignment: Cody Johnston warned us. So. Many. Times.
@bicokun ❤️ 3.0k
Oliver has joined Cody Johnston’s war on boar.
@GryffDavid ❤️ 2.2k
Can't believe I lived to see "30 to 50 feral hogs" guy vindicated with a full lwt story
@maby2911 ❤️ 1.8k
The show missed an epic opportunity to bring Cody Johnston in the crowd for a cameo during John’s speech holding a pitchfork and yelling “yes finally what took you so long”
@Chris-ev5ok ❤️ 1.6k
Cody Johnston called it, multiple times! 😂
@wintercaptain ❤️ 1.1k
I love how all the comments are about Cody and his Showdy. As they should be
@ethanomcbride ❤️ 1.0k
In 2019 I was an intern at the tx state legislature. In a debate over a feral hog bounty hunter bill one rep (Jonathan Stickland) started crying and screaming about how the other members didn’t understand the overwhelming destruction hogs cause in rural communities. It was legitimately the most shocking thing I saw that year and for the Texas state legislature that’s really saying something.
@SupramanRambled ❤️ 1.0k
Ngl, I thought for a second this was a @Some More News episode.

Wake up, Mr. Dr. Cody! The hogs have crossed the event boarizon! They've become real tv news!
@KylarRaynor ❤️ 964
Mad props to lwt writers (hi Dan!) who had to vigorously scour Cody's pig pun list of used jokes and still managed Jurassic Pork
@MrBadSmash ❤️ 746
John Oliver finally catching up to Cody Johnston on the biggest issue of our time
@HiSodiumContent ❤️ 737
If Cody Johnston or the Some More News channel comments here, you guys have to upvote it, trust me, Cody's been going off about wild boars for literal decades.
@TheTimberwolf44 ❤️ 711
The John Oliver/ Cody Johnston crossover the world needs right now
@MarillSweatshirt ❤️ 704
I'm just happy to see people know where to get Some More News
@sabrenawright8646 ❤️ 628
Blanco County tx reported a significant drop in feral hog populations. They discovered that coyotes (who are loosing traditional sources of food) have figured out how to raid piglets when the sow leaves to feed. They use a cunning pack coordinated stealth, stake-out and snatch/kill technique that really elevates understanding of how smart coyotes are. Texas now is considering ways to use this new information.
@laalaa99stl ❤️ 541
I've given up trying to predict what John's topic of the week will be lol
@aabbcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz ❤️ 523
it goes like this
the fourth, the fifth
feral hogs
surround my kids
@cacsims3 ❤️ 496
Last Week Tonight just straight up being Cody Showdy now 😂😂😂
@baval5 ❤️ 432
Oliver is finally aware of the hogs. This is Some News.
@damon22441 ❤️ 420
"If there's one thing a pig is gonna do, it's escalate"
I see what you did there.
@CJPrice2070 ❤️ 420
No f'ing shot. Mr. Cody will blow his lid.
@pyrosianheir ❤️ 379
Cody Johnston punching the air 30-50 times that someone else has finally joined him in bringing us warning about the Hog Menace.
@JohnDoe-yu3em ❤️ 370
Dan Obrien is a sleeper agent for Katie inserting the boar warning into the hbo zeitgeist for the normies
@andreirachko ❤️ 347
Cody Johnston is probably smiling watching this, shout out to the Cody Showdy!
@MandyLorien ❤️ 342
I'm glad people are finally taking Cody Johnston seriously about this threat
@aprilderg7241 ❤️ 323
I used to live in poland where at the time and in the locale it was illegal to hunt our boars since that specific species was endangered. We used to put out a bunch of apples on a tree trunk at the edge of the forest as a sort of offering, and the hogs would only ruin our yard when we forgot to put out the apples. It really gave them the feel of a sort of ancient evil which we must sacrifice to lest our lawn feel the wrath. Really so ironic seeing them make other species endangered instead.
@bambam121691 ❤️ 304
Wormbo, get in here and look at this!!!!
@ancilodon ❤️ 301
One more thing to add: the hogs are eating history, too. At least here in Florida they're known to eat artifacts at and close to the surface. Pot sherds, human and animal bone, etc. As well as the rooting that pigs do severely damages Indian mounds.
@jonathandevries2828 ❤️ 242
Cody Johnston warned us all but we didn't believe him!!!! run!!!!!

@Yovany-d2b ❤️ 1

@luismorenopalacios3441 ❤️ 1

@EphemeralReverie ❤️ 1

@SirYerrin ❤️ 1

@Nokahano_Taris ❤️ 1

@thaBeardedone ❤️ 1
It is from cte that causes lack of impulse control. I know first hand because I suffer from brain atrophy and intracranial pressure due to brain injuries and combat sports. Just pray for him
@aidaarutynian-gagoshidze4594 ❤️ 0
Dustin is going through one of the hardest periods of his life right now. And moments like this can break even the strongest people. Everyone has their own limits, their own battles, their own pain and sometimes life hits all at once.
What he needs now is not judgment, not humiliation, not people tearing him down.
He needs support. He needs understanding. He needs space to breathe and get back on his feet.
You can see it in his eyes he’s exhausted, overwhelmed, and hurting.
And no matter how tough a fighter is in the cage, outside of it he’s still human. With feelings, with struggles, with a heart that gets tired.
I truly hope he finds the strength to get through this chapter.
May life give him the clarity, peace, and support he needs.
May he be surrounded by people who lift him up, not push him deeper.
And may he remember that one difficult moment does not define a whole life.
Everyone falls.
But the strongest are those who rise again.

@King_Yef ❤️ 9.3k
Man I just realized.
The whole reason for this fight, the reason why Flowery has to be in conflict with us, is because the safety of our world is predicated on the destruction of his.

It is, in no uncertain terms, kill or be killed.
@JaydenMatthews-gy2fq ❤️ 7.2k
The prophecy panel tricked us.

It made us think it was Asgore because he was in the picture. But what was he holding?

The golden flower.

The one that became a man.

A flower man.
@Lusiggy ❤️ 7.2k
Everyone: "this Asgore fight boutta be nuts"

Toby: "Twink to C5"

@jerri1918 ❤️ 5.6k

@gellytin ❤️ 5.4k

@26Steps ❤️ 4.7k

@mygemdiamond ❤️ 4.0k
"yes, please keep obeying me!"

The 7 flowers:
@Malo_Rezon ❤️ 3.9k
ralsei stands his ground when he'S downed instead of crumbling, it'S so peak
@Fluumgo ❤️ 3.8k
The virgin flowey kill or be killed mentality
vs
the Chad Flowery Power of Friendship mentality
@Jclubsandwich ❤️ 3.7k
Flowery had a level of self-awareness that none of the other Dark World bosses had. He knew he was just a flower, he knew he was useless for anything but looking good and smelling good and making people happy, so he leaned fully into his persona.

He accepted his role as the "villain" with no reservations, even though he knew that if the fountain stayed open for longer than a few days, he'd die from lack of sunlight in the real world. Even though he was just a flower, he was able to use Dark World shenanigans to lock the Knight in a fight for a few seconds, and he sacrificed his life trying to save Asgore.

He's a remarkably consistent and self-assured character, not motivated by petty hatred like King, ignorance like Queen, or insecurity like Tenna. He's open about his values, defends them against Susie and Ralsei, and stays true to himself until the end. flower king, I kneel!
@spacem80 ❤️ 3.2k
There was only one way to one-up fighting a jrpg final boss in the last chapter, and that was to fight a jrpg protagonist.
@TreeckoBro ❤️ 3.1k
Flowery clashing with the Knight went so damn hard. I thought we were actually going to get a fully animated sprite battle for a sec.
@Bukki13 ❤️ 3.1k
I did not expect lyrics to pop up at the top of the screen
@vvaportarts ❤️ 3.0k
You know it's all serious when ralsei is genuinely pissed off
@rodroHD03 ❤️ 2.9k
Fun fact about the flowers:

Every flower (except flowery) depicts a certain human soul, although flawed in its qualities

-Aqua is supposed to be patience, but they are this impatient, energetic impulsive child, driven by innocent playful desires

-Seth is supposed to be perseverance, and even if they try hard to stop us, they flee at every minor inconvenience, showing a lack of perseverance in their acts

-Yellow is supposed to be justice, but they have a naive, broken morale compass that leads them to make wrong judgements, like jailing themselves for a crime they did not commit

-Blue is supposed to be integrity, although they steal personal objects from other flowers (like the gloves from orange in one of the café's dialogues). Even if they do it for a good purpose, their integrity as a being is flawed as they allow themselves to steal

-Orange is supposed to be bravery, while they hide themselves through shadows and actually have a very fragile personality, taking every comment seriously and seeking help of others instead of facing their problems with bravery

The only one that seems to escape this scope is Green, but as they represent kindness (that describes to do no harm to others and resolve confrontations by giving the other cheek and seeking a peaceful solution) they still rarely attack the group, which although is a bit farfetched, is still not a perfect resemblance of kindness (still, they're incredibly kind, just not as much as they could technically be)

This all resolves with flowery, being the embodiment of all the perfect traits: patience with the cast and their resolution to stop him (as well as a lot of patience with asgore), bravery to face the knight, perseverance in their purpose of keeping the fountain running, integrity as they do not betray their principles at any point of the story, kindness to all the other darkners and even the cast until they have no option but to fight (even not killing the soul after each try), and a fair sense of justice with the debate of which fountain should be closed: ralsei's or theirs.

He is the perfect example of a "human" to their fellow flowers, and acts as a big brother to all of them, trying to teach them the trait that they're supposed to portray, showing that even if his ways were not perfect, flowery always looked down on each and every flower, trying to teach them this new form of life and making them feel more human.
Damn this guy seems like a jerk at first but even in details he is the good guy
@TheBadDeadFox ❤️ 2.9k
The flower cut in half in Tenna's second board was a foreshadowing
@kuroser353 ❤️ 2.9k

@dekucake ❤️ 2.4k

@natsusekai6190 ❤️ 2.4k
me when the power of friendship is fucking awesome actually
@Creeper_945 ❤️ 2.4k
toby fox realized Music 4 wasn't enough so he made Music 5
@Lywooz ❤️ 2.1k
Ralsei was so close to absolutely frying this flower man.
@Lyrune ❤️ 2.0k
Flowery spawning the Floradinns to force the Knight into a battle (which they then couldn't leave, because running from battles literally isn'T A mechanic) was some JoJo level outsmarting! 11/10.
You've earned my respect Flowery, rest in peace.
@abra_escaped ❤️ 1.9k
Incredibly funny how just a few days ago I saw someone speculate that the "flower man" prophecy could mean the flower itself and not asgore and pretty much everyone called it impossible and crazy
@magentajewel ❤️ 1.9k
I love how there isn't really a villain of Chapter 5. Flowery feels very much like the "hero of his own story" -- he has his own found-family of misfit weirdos he's trying to protect, he has so many "power of hope and friendship" moments towards the end of the chapter, and he even has the ability to affect our characters' mercy bars. He feels like someone who, under different circumstances, maybe could have been a friend or ally to our heroes, but is forced into inevitable conflict with them by the contrivances of fate, which adds to the moroseness of the fate which befalls him and the other flowers.
@ethnicjojostani8013 ❤️ 1.8k
You are filled with the power of not knowing what a jarona is.

@idevebydveheud ❤️ 6.3k
The intensity of this song is insane. It's not just a love song, it perfectly captures that heavy longing and the chaos that comes with having a crush.
@luanac8596 ❤️ 3.8k
Even in her more up beat songs, there’s still a hint of sadness in them. I was confused by the album title when it first came out, but Olivia captures it so well. You can be sad and in love. Just beautiful.
@LittleCarpenter11599 ❤️ 3.7k
Those ballerinas are giving brutal vibes. Love it
@Mariamaria-yf4dt ❤️ 3.0k

@VinitaDhakate ❤️ 2.7k

@Several_Bees ❤️ 2.7k

@carloshea15 ❤️ 2.6k
I can’t wait for Olivia Rodrigo to make history again she always kills it
@amjiii23 ❤️ 2.4k
For anyone who's confused:
This is a song about how cats transform into ballerinas.
@taylormullen2815 ❤️ 2.1k
"And if there's a God, he's the bond that's between us two" insane lyric. This song perfectly captures what it's like to fall for someone, and how scary but incredible it is. A small thing, but the ending is really interesting to me. Between the cat pov, the ballerinas, and the scene changes; all of them demonstrate how wonderful but small love makes you feel. The ending shows that this is all in her head. Nobody else can see how she's going crazy, wanting him to feel the same way she does. It's exactly what it's like to be a lover girl with a huge heart, and a bigger imagination.
@cherrykyrene ❤️ 1.9k
I've been repeating drop dead, and the cure...

Now this will probably be on repeat too...
@SarahAnaya-ox3tb ❤️ 1.7k
'i want you more than any stupid song could ever say' probably the realest thing ive ever thought when im in love.
@brparv ❤️ 1.3k
Wow, this music video is how I envision the song to be. The simplicity, mood, the color blue, rain, ballerinas and just everything about it perfectly matches this song. This is masterpiece.
@JesseChibale-eb3xw ❤️ 1.0k
She's not only just singing, she's making art 😭...
@ItsIliana ❤️ 995
she physically cannot make a bad song, this album is incredible 💗
@hangjiandeuli8283 ❤️ 888
Perfectly captures the essence of the album. She wrote it while caught up in the dream of love, but after realizing the rain was only a sprinkler, that he was not the antidote or the cure, and that the angel on the walls of Versailles was just a boy as flawed as anyone else, the album she once filled with love now feels steeped in longing and melancholy. It makes the entire record even more cohesive in retrospect.
@jaco2174 ❤️ 885
I have a theory that when she wrote this song she meant it to be a girl daydreaming of how in love she is. But because of the breakup, it now sounds like she was so deeply in love but that love is gone now and it became such a stupid memory. I'm sure she should've been dancing with the ballerinas something similar to when musicals portray a character who is in love and daydreams of dancing in the street but changed it because of the breakup
@viablanca5651 ❤️ 809
I love the symbolisms here! The ballet dancers becoming "undone" shows a different side of being "unraveled" because of love. The overwhelming feeling of being in love when it's in fact a sprinkler only instead of a rain.
@nishlyn ❤️ 763
I have never heard a song that describe the feeling of wanting someone desperately so accurately 😭😭
@Ava_722 ❤️ 678
I’m so jealous of future me listening to the whole album 💗💄🩰
@wdw_lover ❤️ 641
this was totally worth the wait, she never disappoints in her music

Bro imagine being in the car and seeing freaking Olivia Rodrigo out of the window

olivia’s reoccurring theme is the way she says she isn’t cool.. as she’s doing the coolest shit ever. she is so cool i love her

@thewolfleader500 ❤️ 838.5k

@That_guy102 ❤️ 453.6k

@JBarn7330 ❤️ 167.6k

@doofusmcboobus ❤️ 88.5k
"The worst thing you can do to someone is make them feel unwanted and unloved" hits different after the ribbit backstory
@bubblemagician ❤️ 85.6k
Kaufmo being a normal dude is probably the best Kaufmo we could’ve gotten.
@BruteAndSofty ❤️ 77.8k
"I never programmed you to say you loved me" and then the moon smiling gently back and saying "I know" really got to me
@TheRealBrowknee ❤️ 69.2k
Holy shit the connection between Jax looking at his hand after pushing Pomni in episode 6 and Jax looking at his hand after he pushed his mom was crazy. This show is so peak oml
@1neo.z ❤️ 24.1k
can we appreciate how much the animation, music, and quality of the episodes has changed since the pilot era? Like holy shit this is just phenomenal
@Misaki_somethingidk ❤️ 23.7k
The way it not only started with the first episode's introduction song, but also ended with the same dinner scene, truly a full circle moment🥹🥹 The glitch team did so well on this episode! It was truly the best 3 years.
@BLEH-pu4xe ❤️ 22.9k
And it’s so adorable that kinger was a father and he treated all the circus group as his kids was so cute😭
@Vicus2275 ❤️ 20.9k
Caine wasn't joking when he said that he was gonna show us the most jaw dropping, heart stopping, mind bending paraphernalia that we ever laid our eyes upon...
@CyrusShameliOfficial ❤️ 18.8k
I appreciate the fact the circus members left Jax inside the tent instead of placing him underground in the dark.
@ScarletCarnationRebel ❤️ 15.6k
The ending feels like the fanfiction we’d all read to cope with the real ending, and I’m here for it
@PandorasReachAM ❤️ 13.7k
I really loved the part when they all come hugged and said "we are the amazing digital circus"
@Ur_RobloxBestie ❤️ 13.0k
“Don’t cry because it’s over. Smile because it happened.” no I am crying😭
@MarysLykoCartoons ❤️ 11.8k
It’s like I can already hear the “did you know this in the amazing digital circus?”
@Mefjuu10 ❤️ 10.9k
Congratulations, you survived 2 weeks without spoilers, now you need to survive a few more hours and there you go!
@tastyone1213 ❤️ 10.6k
The end of an era. Thank you for everything, Glitch and Gooseworx.
@Baked_bean571 ❤️ 9.8k
I get that Jax's part is the real gut punch, but Pomni hugging Ragatha, finally giving her the approval she's desperate for, is heartbreakingly bittersweet.
@Clockwork_The_Timeless ❤️ 9.0k
we didn't learn anything new about C&A, but that was the most wholesome ending to a show I have ever seen.
@Thecooldude8070 ❤️ 9.0k
The theme song in the beginning gave me trauma It was like I could still hear Caine's voice 😭 welcome to the amazing digital circus my name is caine I'M your ringmaster...
@user-nf8ip7hu2lHi ❤️ 8.7k
It's a pity that this is the last episode, but I will still love the Amazing Digital circus.
@xirafen ❤️ 8.5k
"This show was made completely by humans!" Love that. Great job from everyone that contributed to the show. <3
@LittleMusicBags11037 ❤️ 8.4k
Kaufmo ringing that doorbell caused the craziest domino effect in history
@SUPER__FLYGOD ❤️ 7.7k

@Toastility ❤️ 7.7k
The table at the end being a circle instead of a long table to show they're more connected as a family is amazing
@rogertrefor9365 ❤️ 7.2k

@MeekandMe ❤️ 54
Im been working in maintenance and construction my whole life. Im 46 now and this absolutely never ever ever happens. The best people get fired, the worst move up and no one does silly tests like this. Trade work can be just as backstabbing, cutthroat, and run by ineptitude and people who only look good on paper. Ive been fired several times in my life for doing the right thing. No one gives a shit. No one will even congratulate you for doing the right thing. But that thats not why you do it in the first place. Maybe people like me are the biggest suckers of them all
@sirsleepy2472 ❤️ 39
This is so awesome. This makes me a little sad to watch because if I’m honest with myself I probably would have lied. 😢
@toonamis2639 ❤️ 34

@BeeManReviews ❤️ 12.9k

@mommytif2920 ❤️ 6.4k

@Jumpman-32 ❤️ 4.0k
She said, “Naw, this requires running, somebody else can do it…”
@JakeKoenig ❤️ 3.0k
I lived a block away from that high school in Tacoma when they were filming this movie. I could look out of my bedroom window and see them filming this scene from my apartment on the 14th floor. I had no idea who any of the actors were at the time so I thought it was a low-budget indie film.
@Buttered_Toast711 ❤️ 2.8k
Also, the arrows aren't pointed so it would've just bounced off him 😂
@KeikilaniKai86 ❤️ 2.1k
I can’t help but to get sad whenever I see clips of this movie. It was filmed at my old high school and neighbors house. One of my teachers was an extra. But, I get sad because I met the whole cast. They were all sweet but Heath and Joseph were by far, still to this very day, 2 of the nicest people I have ever met in my life! They would come say hi and talk to us every time they saw us. They’d have full conversations and be truly invested in those conversations. So, every time I see this movie, I just get sad knowing that the world lost one of the most beautiful souls to ever grace this planet!
@jeffreybeaulieu3219 ❤️ 1.8k
Omg. My life is forever affected by this
@sophiaralston ❤️ 702
As an extras coordinator myself, I‘m delighted to see this😂
@Miss_nice74 ❤️ 331
She likely thought she was off screen 😂😂😂😂
@JThreefingers-ms3kd ❤️ 255
Not her favorite teacher. Probably turned around to enjoy watching him roll around in pain.
@lordbob1364 ❤️ 223
I wonder if it's an aspect ratio thing. Idk about with movies, but there was a time in television when we were switching from standard format to widescreen format that they would film in wide screen, but all the shots were framed to be in standard. I see it a lot in the later seasons of Buffy The Vampire Slayer, where you can tell you're seeing stuff not intended to make it to tv, like actors standing to one side not really emoting in a way that makes sense for the scene, cuz all you would have scene on tv was part of their shoulder or something.
@samantha490 ❤️ 162
Maybe she actually didn’t want to help the teacher lmao
@PieterAdriaanvanderWalt ❤️ 162
They teach you in First Aid if you're helping somebody and you need to get a bystander to call the ambulance, you got to ask atleast 2, preferably 3 to call.

1. You may have a caller just walk away not wanting to be part of this
2. The ambulance getting 2 or 3 calls for the same incident knows it's more likely to be real and not just a prank.
@skywizard3148 ❤️ 127
The first time I noticed a mess up was in Braveheart. An English soldier is swinging his sword at 1/8th speed, just going through the motions
